name: Python
description: A versatile beginner-friendly language used in web development, data science, automation, and AI.

lessons.title: Hello World & Print
lessons.content: The print() function displays output to the screen. It is the first step in any Python program. You can print text (strings), numbers, and even results of expressions.
lessons.code_example:
print('Hello, World!')
print(42)
print(3.14)
print('My name is', 'Alice')

exercises.question: Print the text 'I am learning Python'.
hints: Use print() with the text in quotes

exercises.question: Which function displays output in Python?
hints: It is a built-in Python function

lessons.title: Variables & Data Types
lessons.content: Variables store data values. Python has several built-in data types: int (integers), float (decimals), str (text), and bool (True/False). You don't need to declare the type — Python detects it automatically.
lessons.code_example:
name = 'Alice'
age = 25
height = 1.75
is_student = True

print(type(name))    # <class 'str'>
print(type(age))     # <class 'int'>

exercises.question: Create a variable called 'city' with the value 'Agadir' and print it.
hints: Use = to assign a value to a variable
Then use print() to display it

exercises.question: What data type is the value True in Python?
hints: It represents a logical true/false value

lessons.title: Conditions & If Statements
lessons.content: Conditions allow your program to make decisions. Use if, elif, and else to control the flow. Comparison operators: == (equal), != (not equal), > (greater than), < (less than).

exercises.question: What keyword is used for an alternative condition in Python?
hints: It is a shorthand for 'else if'
